channel.title: +दुष्यन्तः on रामानुजीये विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +दुष्यन्तः on रामानुजीये विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: HanumAn hero
item.description:
Source: TW

At times, I feel rejuvenated looking at a fresh set of astikas, many of them between the age of 10 & 25 (mainly born after 2000) across both the genders and castes attending discourses, not just in metros but even in Tier-1 & 2 towns. I have wondered as to ‘why this new found love for lectures’ when there are other ways for them to spend time. This I realised when I could pick some of their minds. Their questions like ‘Who is Bhagavan? How can I know more about him? What are the simple ways that I can intersperse in my daily life with, that will give me peace? Why should I come to a temple?’ are answered when speakers deliver their talks. Today, I can vouch that the number of astikas who attend my lectures are across the entire spectrum of the society.

item.title: Trad vs modern
item.description:
Source: TW

5. There is another issue in his tweet. Not just him. A few others like him. They will always say traditional vs modern. If say, the upanyasaka is between 50 & 90 years, somehow they will be right is the thought. I humbly differ. I have heard 1000s of discourses in Tamizh, Kannada, Telugu of atleast 70+ speakers born in the last 100+ years. I like each of them for a variety of reasons. Delivery, voice, diction, examples etc. Earlier I used to research on their lives, so that I can take cues from them. To my shock and disappointment, I found some of them, their preachings opposite to their living standards. With no malice, just telling a few::
